def: s.m. Hot vapour, steam; evaporation
subs: v.t. To take a vapour bath; to inhale steam.

def: adj. Seventy-nine.
adj. High, &c. = اونچا ūṅćā, q.v.
adj. (f. -ī), High, lofty, tall, raised, elevated, exalted, eminent; steep; above, upper; loud (as a voice)
subs: uṅć-nīć, adj. High and low, having ups and downs, uneven, rough; undulatory; — s.f. Unevenness, inequality, undulating ground; ups and downs (of life), vicissitudes; ins and outs, pros and cons (of a matter), particulars
v.n. To speak in a loud voice; to talk proudly or boldly
v.n. To mount high, ascend or soar; to be high up; to rise to eminence, attain to high rank
ūṅćā sunāʼī denā, v.n. To be hard of hearing
v.t. To raise, hoist, uplift, exalt, elevate
(-kā), To have (one's) power, affluence, &c. continued to one (to be always in a position to give), to remain or continue in the position of a lord or superior; to have the upper hand; to excel
s.f. Loud voice or tone; high note
s.f. A grand or fine shop; great show
ūṅćī lenā, To sound one's own praises; to praise one's own wares; to demand a high price
The proud ones are brought low
To sing in a high key.

def: adj. High, &c. = اونچا ūṅćā, q.v.
adj. (f. -ī), High, lofty, tall, raised, elevated, exalted, eminent; steep; above, upper; loud (as a voice)
subs: uṅć-nīć, adj. High and low, having ups and downs, uneven, rough; undulatory; — s.f. Unevenness, inequality, undulating ground; ups and downs (of life), vicissitudes; ins and outs, pros and cons (of a matter), particulars
v.n. To speak in a loud voice; to talk proudly or boldly
v.n. To mount high, ascend or soar; to be high up; to rise to eminence, attain to high rank
ūṅćā sunāʼī denā, v.n. To be hard of hearing
v.t. To raise, hoist, uplift, exalt, elevate
(-kā), To have (one's) power, affluence, &c. continued to one (to be always in a position to give), to remain or continue in the position of a lord or superior; to have the upper hand; to excel
s.f. Loud voice or tone; high note
s.f. A grand or fine shop; great show
ūṅćī lenā, To sound one's own praises; to praise one's own wares; to demand a high price

To sing in a high key.
ex: تصویر کو تھوڑا اور اونچا کرو۔
Move the picture a little higher.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀉𑀘𑁆𑀘 (ucca), from Sanskrit उच्च (ucca). Doublet of اچ, a tatsama. Cognate with Punjabi ਉੱਚ (ucca) / اُچّ (ucc), Gujarati ઉંચું (uñcũ), Marathi उंच (uñca), Bengali উঁচু (ũcu), Dhivehi އުސް (us).

def: s.f. = H انچاو उंचाव uṅćāʼo, s.m. = H انچائی उंचाई uṅćāʼī, s.f. (from ūṅćā, q.v.) Height, loftiness, elevation, altitude; eminence, exaltation; pitch (of the voice).
s.f. Height, &c. = uṅćāʼī, q.v. (syn. are uṅćās, uṅćān, uṅćāʼo, uṅćāhaṭ).
s.f. = H انچاو उंचाव uṅćāʼo, s.m. = H انچاهٿ उंचाहट uṅćāhaṭ, s.f. (from ūṅćā, q.v.) Height, loftiness, elevation, altitude; eminence, exaltation; pitch (of the voice).
adj. = untālīs, q.v.
s.m.f. = uṅćāʼī, q.v.
v.t. To raise, elevate, hoist, &c. = ūṅćānā, and ūṅćā karnā, q.q.v.
v.t. To raise, &c. = ūṅćā karnā, q.v.s.v. ūṅćā.
s.m. = H انچاهٿ उंचाहट uṅćāhaṭ, s.f. = H انچائی उंचाई uṅćāʼī, s.f. (from ūṅćā, q.v.) Height, loftiness, elevation, altitude; eminence, exaltation; pitch (of the voice).
adj. Forty-nine.
s.f. = uṅćāʼī, q.v.
v.t. To comb (the hair).
v.t. = ućkānā, q.v.
v.s.v. ūṅć.
v.t. = unḍelnā, q.v.
v.t. = unḍālnā and unḍelnā, q.q.v.
v.t. To turn (a vessel) upside down in order to empty it; to empty, to pour out (the contents of a vessel).
v.t. To cause to be poured out, to have or get (a vessel) emptied, or (the contents) poured out.
s.m. The first, or the beginning (of a thing); the prime, spring, or bloom (of youth); fresh vigour.
s.f. = ūṅgh, q.v.
s.m. = uṅgnī, q.v.
s.f. = ūṅgh, q.v.
s.f. Dropping of the head or eye-lid, nodding, dozing, drowsiness, sleepiness ( = uṅghās, uṅghāʼī, q.q.v.).
s.f. Drowsiness, sleepiness, dozing, heaviness, dulness ( = ūṅgh, q.v.).
v.n. To be drowsy, to doze, &c. = ūṅghnā, q.v.
s.f. = uṅghāʼī and ūṅgh, q.q.v.

ety: S. उद्, or व्या+घारणीयं, rt. घृ
def: v.t. To apply grease or oil, mixed with tar, &c., to the axle of a cart or carriage; to grease the wheels of a cart, &c.
v.n. To drop the head, to nod, doze, feel drowsy or sleepy.
adj. (f. -ī), Dozing; drowsy, sleepy; dull, stupid; sluggish
subs: adj. Drowsy, frowsy, dull and stupid; unaccompanied by any show or pomp, quiet (as a wedding, &c.).

def: v.n. = ūṅghnā, q.v.
s.f. Drowsiness, &c. = uṅghāʼī (the more com. form, q.v.); — greasing the wheels of a cart or carriage. (See ūṅghnā, ūṅgnā.)
( = un), obl. pl. of the pron. وه wǒh, 'that'; 'he.'
intj. Pshaw! no matter! plague take it! &c. (cf. next).
s.f. Manner, fashion, appearance; — adj. Like, resembling, similar.
adj. Sixty-nine.
adv. There and then; exactly in that manner, so.
intj. Not I! nay! no! never! humph! very nice! very fine!
adj. Made of wool, woollen.
adj. (f. -ī), Sleepless, awake; unclosed (eye); expanded (as a flower).
adj. = ujālā, q.v.
s.f. A small heap of corn set apart (by Musalmans) in harvest time in the name of some saint.
s.m. Light = ujālā, q.v.
s.m. Light, &c. = ujālā, q.v.
s.f. Curve, crook, bend; a curved line after a figure (e.g. را four annas'; راا 'eight annas'
subs: without this curved line the marks would denote 'one pice

def: s.m. Madness, mania; extravagance.
adj. Insane, frantic, mad; drunk, intoxicated; furious; lustful; extravagant; — s.m. Lunatic, &c.
adj. (f. -ī), Mad, &c. = unmatta, q.v.
s.f. Measure (of size or quantity), length, size, dimension, magnitude; duration, period.
s.m. Cloud; clouds.
adj. Insane, frantic, mad; drunk, intoxicated; furious; lustful; extravagant; — s.m. Lunatic, &c.
s.f. Madness; intoxication; fury; lust.
s.m. Opening the eyes, looking at; perception; winking, twinkling of the eyelids.
adj. Raising the face, looking up or at.
s.m. The jujube fruit (and tree), Rhamnus zizyphus.
adj. Of the colour of the jujube, jujube-colour, red, of a carnation colour.
part. adj. Elevated; high, tall; — s.m. Height, elevation ( = next, q.v.)
s.f. Elevation, height; rising, ascending; advancement, exaltation; increase, prosperity.
adj. Nineteen
subs: adv. As nineteen and twenty, but slightly different
adj. Having the difference of nineteen and twenty, but slightly different, nearly equal
To be but slightly different, to be nearly alike, equal, &c.; to have the chances (for or against) pretty equally balanced; — to chance, happen, betide, befall.

def: adj. Nineteenth.
s.m. Sociableness; companionableness; familiarity, friendliness, friendship, love, affection; society, companionship, fellowship; cheerfulness.
adj. Fifty-nine.
adj. Fifty-nine.
adj. Ninety-nine. (The more common term for 'ninety-nine' is ninnānwe.)
= A انثيان uns̤ayān, s.m. dual (of انثی), The two testicles; the two ears.
= A انثيين uns̤ayain, s.m. dual (of انثی), The two testicles; the two ears.
s.f. Familiarity; state of being domesticated, tameness (opp. to waḥshīyat); companionableness, sociableness, &c. ( = uns, q.v.).
s.m. A squill (syn. isqīl).
s.m. Origin (syn. aṣl); a primary element.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عُنْصُر ('unsur), from Arabic عُنْصُر (ʕunṣur).

def: adj. Original, elemental, elementary.
s.m. Camel = ūṅt, q.v.
s.m. Camel
subs: To make a camel lie down in order to load it
(prov.) 'The dog bites one who is mounted on a camel,' i.e. Misfortune has long arms for the unfortunate
s.m. A thistle of which camels are fond, Echinops echinatus

def: adj. Thirty-nine.
s.m. Three and a half; three and a half times ( = hūṅṭhā).
adj. Twenty-nine.
s.f. A she-camel.
s.f. (in Gram.), A diminutive noun.

ety: orig. ʻunnān, v.n. fr. عنّ 'to occur before,' &c.
def: s.m. Superscription, title, or title-page (of a book, &c.); preface; anything that serves as an indication (of another thing); that which is understood (by anything); — mode, manner.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عُنْوَان (unwān), itself from Arabic عُنْوَان (ʕunwān).

def: An inseparable Sanskrit prefix denoting vicinity or proximity, — towards, near to; down, under; on; by, by the side of; with, by means of, &c.
s.m. A grove; an artificially planted wood; a garden.
s.m. Service, attendance; remedy; proceeding, practice, usage; profession; the practice of medicine; a present; a bribe.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Attending, serving, &c.; — a servant.
s.f. A meeting; a present made at an interview; a bribe.
s.m. Taking away; abduction; seizure; withdrawing the organs of sense and perception from the outer world; abstraction; — cause, motive.
s.m. A relish; flavour; anything to excite drinking or appetite; — the venereal disease.
s.m. Spectacles.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā), Door-keeping; a door-keeper; spectacles; a wink.
s.m. A commentary.
s.m. = اپدرو upadrav, q.v.
s.m. Advice, counsel, exhortation, admonition, instruction, preaching, sermon; direction; suggestion. — upadeś karnā, v.t. To counsel, exhort, &c.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā), Instructing, instructive, didactic; — adviser, instructor, admonisher, preacher.
s.m. (f. -trī), An adviser, a teacher; a spiritual guide.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī and -in), Advising, instructing; — adviser, teacher, preacher.
v.t. = upadeś karnā, q.v. s.v. upadeś.
s.f. Violence, injury, injustice, tyranny, oppression, exaction, trouble, annoyance, mischief, disturbance. — upādhi-se, adv. Unjustly; tyrannically.
s.m. That upon which one rests; a pillow, bolster, cushion; — affection, kindness; — religious observance or obligation; poison.

s.m. An inferior mineral, a semi-metal (e.g. pyrites, sulphate of copper, talc, antimony, red orpiment, yellow orpiment, calx of brass); — secondary secretion of the body (such as milk, menses, adeps, sweat, teeth, hair, lymph).
s.m. Virtuous reflection; — deceit, deception; disguise; — discriminating or distinguishing property, attribute, peculiarity; modifying circumstance; limitation; — a title, nickname.
adj. (f. -inī), Violent, unjust, tyrannical
s.f. The wife of a teacher; a preceptress (see next).
s.m. A spiritual preceptor; a teacher (in general).
s.f. = اپادهيانی upādhyānī, q.v.
s.f. = اپادهيانی upādhyānī, q.v.
s.m. A peninsula.

ety: also, in H. उपद्रो upadro, and उपद्रौ upadrau
def: s.m. A natural phenomenon considered as a portent; a portent, a sudden calamity; commotion, disturbance, tumult, rebellion; violence, outrage, excess, injustice, tyranny, oppression, wrong, injury; insult.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Portentous; turbulent, violent, factious, rebellions; unjust, tyrannical, oppressive
= اپدروي upadravī, q.v. (cf. upaddar.)
s.m. A peninsula.
s.m. Approach, approximation; attachment; intercourse; agreement; promise.
part. adj. (f. -ā), Approached, drawn near, attached (to); obtained; promised; agreed.
s.f. Going near; approach.
s.m. A prisoner, a man or animal in confinement; — encouragement; favour; assistance.
s.m. Interview; complimentary gift, present to a superior; offering to a deity; adoration; religious exultation.
ex: میں نے اسے ایک قیمتی گاڑی اُپہار دیا.
I gave him a costy car as a gift.

def: s.m. Laughter, fun, sport; ridicule, derision. — upahās karnā (-kā), To laugh (at), deride, ridicule, &c.

s.f. Produce, crop, product, yield; original idea, invention; power of perception or thought; flight of fancy; wit; anything spoken or sung extempore; the burden of a song, the chorus; variations in music.
s.m. Preparation; exertion.

ety: an irr. form. of the Hindī; the S. form is upajāta
def: part. adj. (f. -ā), Produced; engendered; propagated.
s.m. = اپجيوکا upajīvikā.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Dependent, living by or on; a dependent.
s.f. Means of support, livelihood, subsistence.

ety: upaj˚ = Prk. उप्पज्ज(इ) = S. उत्पद्य(ते), rt. उद्+पद्
def: v.n. To grow, be produced, spring up, shoot, sprout; to be born; to originate; to exist, be, have being; to happen, to become visible, to appear; to occur (to the mind), to be suggested, spring up (in the mind).

s.m. Favour, kindness, benefit, beneficence, goodness; assistance; preservation, protection. — upakārtha, adv. For the sake of, or with a view to, assistance, &c. — upakār karnā (-kā), To help, befriend, &c.
s.m. (f. -ikā), Helper; benefactor, friend; protector.
s.m. The insignia of royalty; — means, materials, implements; a helping or assisting.

adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Aiding, assisting; beneficent, good, kind, friendly; philanthropic; helpful, subsidiary, subservient, serviceable; instrumental, conducive (to); — helper, benefactor, beneficent man, philanthropist.
s.m. A legend; a tale.
s.m. Deliberate commencement of an undertaking; beginning; undertaking, enterprise; stratagem.
s.m. Rock, stone; precious stone, jewel.
s.m. A figure of speech; metaphor; synecdoche; elliptical expression.
s.m. Abuse; reviling; — delaying.
s.m. = S اپليپن उपलेपन upalepan, s.m. Anointing, smearing, plastering; plastering with cowdung.
s.m. = S اپليپ उपलेप upalep, s.m. Anointing, smearing, plastering; plastering with cowdung.
part. adj. (f. -ā), Smeared, plastered.
= H اپلچهن अपलच्छण apalaććhaṇ, = H اپلچهنا अपलच्छणा apalaććhaṇā, Corr. of the three following words, qq.v.

s.f. = S اپمان उपमान upamān (and upamāṅ), s.m. Resemblance, similarity, likeness; counterpart, fellow; analogy; comparison; simile, metaphor; that to which another thing is likened. — upamā-denā (-se), To compare (with); to liken (to), &c.
s.f. Simile, &c. = upamā, q.v.

(and upamāṅ), s.m. = S اپما उपमा upamā, s.f. Resemblance, similarity, likeness; counterpart, fellow; analogy; comparison; simile, metaphor; that to which another thing is likened. — upamā-denā (-se), To compare (with); to liken (to), &c.
= اپما upamā, and also = اپميه upameya, qq.v.
part. adj. To be compared; comparable.
s.f. Resemblance, likeness, image, picture; comparison, analogy; induction.
v.t. To get, obtain, earn, produce, create; to prepare; to devise, contrive, adopt (as an experiment or remedy).
s.m. Title; family name, surname; nickname. — upanām-vāćī-śabd, s.m. Any word indicative of a title or family name.
adj. Having the title of; surnamed; nicknamed.

s.m. Bringing near; supplying; employing; initiation; the initiation of the three first classes of Hindūs; investiture with the sacred thread (com. called janeü, q.v.) worn over the left shoulder and under the right.

s.m. Bringing near; supplying; employing; initiation; the initiation of the three first classes of Hindūs; investiture with the sacred thread (com. called janeü, q.v.) worn over the left shoulder and under the right.
s.m. Eye-glasses, spectacles.
s.m. The sectarial mark made with sandal, &c. on the forehead.
s.m. A kind of musical instrument.
s.m. Sacred texts or extracts from the Vedas; the theological and argumentative parts of the Vedas.
s.m. Intrusting, giving; pretext; declaration; precept, law; prologue, tale.
s.m. = اپاي upāy, q.v.
s.m. A crime or sin in the second degree (one that can be expiated), such as killing a cow, selling a daughter, atheism, &c.
s.m. A paramour; a gallant; one who keeps a woman to whom he is not married.
s.f. Proof, demonstration.
s.m. Pulling up or out by the root, &c. (see the next); — an irritating composition (as a blister, or the like).
adv. Over, by; without effect, harmlessly; not thoroughly, superficially, cursorily, hastily ( = ūpar-hī-ūpar)
subs: adv. Skimming the surface; superficially; with a light hand, gently
adv. Upon, upon or after another, in succession
adv. From above, over, outside, externally, superficially; in addition, besides, to boot
adj. (f. -ī), Outer, external, superficial, &c. ( = ūparī, q.v.); higher, upper; superior, best; preceding, foregoing
To make a show of affection, to feign regard; to breathe one's last
s.m. Miscellaneous duties, odd jobs or services
s.f. Emoluments, perquisites, presents (and such-like illegal sources of income)
(apnī), To take upon (oneself), to undertake, engage, become responsible for

s.m. The Being above, The Supreme Being; the sky above; canopy of clouds; the moon above; — ūpar-wāle, s.m. pl. Superiors, betters; those in high position; the lodgers or dwellers in the upper story (of a house); those who have gone before, predecessors, forefathers; upper servants; personal attendants; ministers, stewards; outsiders, strangers
s.f. pl. (In the language of women), the spirits above, fairies, evil spirits; the fowls of the air, kites and crows
adv. On the surface; superficially, externally ( = ūpar-ūpar); apart, alone; secretly, quietly, stealthily.

def: postpos. (governing the inflected gen. masc.), Above, upon, on, over, on the outside (of); moreover, concerning, on account (of).
s.m. Darkening; an eclipse; calamity; colour, painting.
v.t. To create, produce, cause to grow or spring forth, to cultivate.
postpos. (governing the inflect. gen. masc.), After (of time); afterwards; over and above, besides
s.m. A secondary mineral, as red chalk, bitumen, &c.; a secondary passion or feeling; a subordinate flavour.

ety: S. उपरि + आ + छाट्, the towel being used to cover the body
def: s.m. A towel or cloth for wiping the body
subs: (This and a number of other words seem to point to a Braj form उपरौं = उपरि).

ety: S. उपरि + आ + छाट्, the towel being used to cover the body
def: s.m. A towel or cloth for wiping the body
subs: (This and a number of other words seem to point to a Braj form उपरौं = उपरि).

def: adj. Upper, outer, &c. ( = اوپري ūprī, q.v.). — uparī man-se kahnā, To say what is not one's real thought or feeling.

adj. Upper, outer, external, superficial, hasty, cursory, not thorough, not sound or strong, &c.; unreal, specious, feigned; ostensible, apparent; belonging to another or to without, unfamiliar, strange, foreign; — s.m. Outsider, stranger, foreigner; exotic; that which is over and above, overplus, surplus; fees, perquisites (cf. ūpar-kī āmad).
s.m. The being above or higher; eminence, exaltation, dignity, high position.
s.m. Acquiring, gaining, earning; acquisition, gain. — upārjan karnā, v.t. To acquire, &c.
part. adj. Acquired, gained, earned.
adj. (f. -ī), Outer; upper; exterior; superficial; — s.m. the surface (cf. uprālā).
adj. & s.m. See اپرلا uparlā.
v.t. = اپاڙنا upāṛnā, q.v.
v.t. To pullup or out by the root, to root up, eradicate, tear or pluck out, extirpate.
s.m. A covering worn loosely over the shoulders; a scarf.
v.n. To be rooted out, torn up, pulled or plucked out; to be skinned; (see the tr. form upāṛnā); to come or rise up, to appear; to be marked, impressed, imprinted (with the mark of anything).
s.m. Obstacle, impediment; disturbance; injury; — partiality; protection, support, favour, kindness.
s.m. Obstruction, impediment, obstacle.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Obstructing, impeding; disturbing; — impediment, obstacle, &c.
s.m. Chief priest of a Hindū temple.

ety: v.n. from caus. from उपरि+v.n. from caus. from rt. तृप्
def: s.f. Surfeit; mort de chien, cholera morbus.
s.m. A fast; fasting; hunger. — upās karnā, v.n. To fast, abstain from eating and drinking.
s.f. Worship, &c. — upās karnā, v.t. To worship, make offerings to, sacrifice to.
s.f. Smell, stink; staleness, mustiness; putrefaction, rottenness.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Fasting; hungry; — one who fasts.
s.f. Service, worship; religious meditation.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā). Fasting, &c. = اپاسا upāsā, q.v.
s.m. (f. -ikā), A servant, a worshipper.
s.m. Patience, calmness.
s.m. Sitting by the side of; attending upon, serving; worship, adoration; religious meditation; — the sacred fire; archery.
s.f. Service, attendance; reverence, worship, adoration; offering, sacrifice.
adj. Calm, pacified, appeased.
s.f. Tranquillity, calm; — adj. Appeasing, allaying.
s.m. (f. -inī), Appeased, tranquil, calm; tame; — one who is calm or tranquil.
s.m. A portent, a natural phenomenon supposed to forebode future evil; a disease, possession by an evil spirit; (in gram.) a particle prefixed to roots, &c., a prefix; a preposition.
s.m. Sciences connected with or derived from the śāstras.
s.m.The right side or half of the body,the right: — adj.opposite.contrary.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Serving; worshipping; — a worshipper; a devotee.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Fasting, hungry; — one who observes a fast. — upāsī-paṛnā, To lie down fasting, go to bed hungry.
v.t. To serve, worship, reverence, adore.
v.n. = ابسنا ubasnā, q.v.
s.m. The organs of generation of a man or woman; the anus; the haunches; the legs; the arms.
part. adj. Arrived; ready, present; at hand, near, impending. — upasthit karnā, v.t. To summon; to present, &c.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Worthy of service, reverence, or worship; — an object of reverence, &c.
s.m. Heat, warmth; pain, trouble, misfortune; sickness, disease; haste, hurry.
v.n. To be tired, to be sick or weary of a business.
v.t. = اپاڙنا upāṛnā, q.v.

ety: S. उद्+पतनं, but cf. ubaṭnā and ubalnā
def: v.n. To run over, overflow, be excessive; to fall off, be wasted; to be removed, to be taken away
s.m. Censure, blame.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Censuring, blaming; — censurer, &c.
part. adj. (f. -ā), Censured, blamed, accused, complained against; forbidden.
s.m. Grove; artificially planted wood (see upaban).
s.m. Minute description.
s.m. Fasting; a fast; hunger, starvation. — upavās karnā, To fast.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit उपवास (upavāsa). Doublet of اُپاس (upās).

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Fasting, observing a fast; — one who fasts.
s.m. A class of writings (four in number) deduced from, and subordinate to, the four Vedas.
s.f. Profane science (as opposed to sacred); inferior kind of knowledge.
s.m. A factitious poison; a narcotic; a deleterious drug (as opium, dhatūra, &c.).
s.m. The thread worn by the first three classes of Hindūs over the left shoulder and under the right arm. (See janeü.)

s.m. Scheme, plan, means, expedient, device, contrivance, stratagem; way, course, measure; remedy, resource; preparation. upāʼe karnā, v.n. To devise, plan, plot, scheme; to try an expedient, &c., to adopt a measure.
adj. Contriving, remedying, &c. (see upāy)
s.m. An interview; present made at an interview.
s.m. Husband, lord.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Contriving, resorting to expedients, remedying; expert in the use of means; — an expert in devising, &c.
s.m. Application; employment, service, use; suitableness.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Useful, suitable; — a useful person.
part. adj. (f. -ā), Worthy, fit, proper, becoming, right; qualified; adapted to, suitable, appropriate.
s.f. Suitableness, fitness, &c.
s.f. = اپيکشا upekshā, q.v.
s.f. Neglect; indifference; contempt; abandonment; trick, stratagem, deceit (as one of the minor expodients in war).
= اپاڙنا upāṛnā, q.v.

ety: S. उद्+v.n. from rt. स्फाय; cf. फेन
def: s.m. Ebullition; froth, foam; effervescence.
v.n. To boil up, to boil over; to bubble; foam, froth, effervesce; to ferment; to turn bad (as milk, &c.); — to foam with rage, to fume. — uphan jānā, v.n. = uphannā.
v.t. To cause to grow, to produce, cultivate; to create; suggest (an idea); to fabricate, invent.
adj. Fertile, &c. = اپجاو upjāʼū, q.v.
adj. Fertile, productive, rich.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā), Producing, creating; — a producer, &c.
s.m. (f. -ī), Dried cakes of cowdung (used for fuel).
v.n. To spring or jump up; to rise suddenly (as a float after being pressed down under a fluid); to rise above the surface (of water), to float.
s.f. dim. of uplā, q.v.
adv. Over, above. (See next).
adj. (f. -ī), Upper, higher; uppermost, highest; exterior, outer; — s.m. being by the side (of), taking the part (of)
subs: (-kā), To take the part (of), to side (with), help, support, protect; to take part (in) officiously or obtrusively; to meddle, interfere.

def: Lit. 'Standing on the other or opposite side'; the west (cf aprauṭhā).
adj. (f. -ī), Upper, higher; uppermost, highest. — uprauṭhī koṭhṛī, An upper room.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Meddlesome, officious, obtrusive; intrusive; quarrelsome, wrangling; — a meddlesome person; a quarrelsome person.
= ابسانا ubsānā, q.v.
(corr. apṭan) = ابٿن ubṭan, q.v.
= ابٿنا ubṭanā, and ابٿن ubṭan, qq.v.
= ابٿن ubṭan, q.v.
s.m. Breast, bosom; heart
subs: — meṅ laga-lenā, v.t. To clasp to the bosom, embrace, caress, fondle.

ety: pl. of اعور aʻwar, v.n. fr. عور 'to be blind of one eye,' &c.
def: adj. One-eyed, monocular (syn. kānā); — naked, nude, bare ( = ʻuryān).
s.m. Lack, scarcity, want = toṛā, q.v.
s.m. A kind of pulse, Dolichos pilosus
subs: v.t. To throw uṛad (at one) after pronouncing an incantation over it; to cast a spell over; to enchant, exorcise
adv. (As much as) the white spot on uṛad, the least bit, the smallest particle.

def: s.m. pl. (of عريف ʻarīf, syn. of ʻārif, q.v.), The wise, the holy, those who are skilled in divine things; the intelligent, the ingenious; judges.
s.m. Snake reptile, worm
subs: (ʻsnake-eater'), s.m. (in Myth.) Garuḍ, the regent of birds
s.m. The infernal regions, the abode of the Nāgas, Pātāla.

def: v.n. local, for الجهنا ulajhnā, q.v.

ety: उड़, rt. of उड़ना+āk = S. aff. āka
def: adj. Capable of flying; fledged; flier.
s.f. The act of flying; flight
subs: s.m. A boys' game (resembling our 'ring-taw,' but played with cowries instead of marbles: syn. jhallā-mīrī)
(ćhū = S. द्रुत, 'presto!' or is onomat.), s.m. Flying away; disappearing; instantaneous flight
v.n. To fly away; to disappear, vanish, be non est
s.f. Trickery, deception, trick, ruse, stratagem; evasion, shuffling
v.t. To deceive, trick, gammon; to evade, shuffle
(-se), To fly (from), be averse (to), sick (of), tired (of).

def: adj. Flying; raging, spreading, &c. (used in comp.)
subs: s.m. A kind of firework; a kind of rocket
s.f. An infectious or contagious disease; epidemic
s.m. The flying car of fairy tales which moves through the air at the will of the rider; a small ornamental rocking bed for children; a rough litter, a bier (of the kind used by Hindūs).

def: v.t. To cause to fly; to fly, let fly; to throw or toss up (as a pigeon, or a coin); to set free, uncage; to cause to move rapidly or swiftly, to drive hard (a horse, &c.); to fly away with; to run or make off with; to make away with, to rob, plunder, despoil of; to ravish; to cause to vanish or disappear; to squander, dissipate, waste; to put out of sight, hide, conceal (from, -se, as a secret, &c.); to use, apply, or handle dexterously, skilfully or effectively (as an instrument, &c.); to puff or blow away; to scatter, disperse; to winnow; to drive away, frighten away, scare; to blow up, explode (as a fort, mine, &c.); to destroy; to spread abroad, circulate (a rumour); to unfold, unfurl (a sail, flag, &c.); to give the reins to, give free scope to, indulge in (pleasure, e.g. mazā uṛānā); to knock off, cut off (the head); to strike out, wipe out, efface, obliterate; to put away from, cast to the winds, disregard (advice, &c.); to get rid of, send away, dismiss; to hurl or throw down (in wrestling); to knock over, send flying; to take off, copy, mimic; to play upon, deceive, cheat, befool, gammon
subs: ( = uṛānā, but generally intensive, e.g.), To carry off or away; to drive or frighten away; to give or cast to the winds; to wipe clean out, completely efface; to blow away; to blow down; to blow up, cause to blaze up; to explode (a mine); to get rid of, dismiss, &c.
uṛā le-jānā, v.t. To make off with, run away with, carry off; to kidnap; to entice away
v.t. To carry off, plunder, steal, rob.

def: s.m. Breast, bosom, chest
subs: s.m. Lit. "Produced on the chest"; the female breast.

def: s.m. A bug.
v.t. To stick in, infix, insert; to prick, pierce, penetrate; to tuck in, tuck up, fold in; to stuff in, to poke in, plug

ety: uṛā, rt. of uṛānā+ū = S. aff. उक
def: s.m. Extravagant person, spendthrift, prodigal
s.f. Fertile soil, yielding every kind of crop.
s.f. Fertile soil, yielding every kind of crop.
s.f. Name of an Apsara or nymph of Indra's heaven; an apparition, phantom; an ornament worn on the breast.
v.n. v.s.v. اڙنا uṛnā.
s.m. A kind of vetch, Dolichos pilosus.
s.f. An armed female attendant in a harem.
adj. Raised, erected, erect, upright; above, high, superior, upper
subs: adj. Having the arms raised; — s.m. A devotee who holds one or both arms above his head constantly till they are fixed in that position
(S. ūrdhwa-puṇḍra), s.m. A perpendicular line made with sandal on the forehead by Vaishnavas or Brāhmans who are worshippers of Vishṇu
s.m. A kind of ascetic or rishi who lives in perpetual chastity
s.m. Expiration, gasp, deep sigh, hard breathing (as in asthma)
s.m. Going upwards, ascending; ascent; stiff walk or gait, walking proudly; — ūrdh-gaman karnā, To ascend, mount up, &c.
adj. Having the face raised, with upturned face

def: s.m. A kind of vetch ( = urad, q.v.).
s.m. A disease occasioned by the mounting of wind upwards; flatulency; &c. ( = uddhak, q.v.).
s.m. A disease occasioned by the mounting of wind upwards; flatulency; &c. ( = uddhak, q.v.).
s.f. The second month of the solar year (see urdě-bihisht); the first month in autumn.
s.f. Uniform; livery; appointed dress of the subordinate employés of any department.
s.m. An otter (com. form ūd-bilāʼo).

s.m. Army; camp; market of a camp; s.f. ( = urdū zabān), The Hindūstānī language as spoken by the Muhammadans of India, and by Hindūs who have intercourse with them or who hold appointments in the Government courts, &c. (It is composed of Hindī, Arabic, and Persian, Hindī constituting the back-bone, so to speak)
subs: The royal camp or army (generally means the city of Dehli or Shāhjahānābād); the court language ( = urdū-i-muʻallā-kī zabān); the Hindūstānī language as spoken in Dehli.
wety: Shortened from اُرْدُوئے مُعَلّیٰ (urdūe mu'allā), from زُبانِ اُرْدُوئے مُعَلّیٰ (zubān-i urdūe mu'allā), borrowed from Classical Persian زُبَانِ اُرْدُویِ مُعَلّیٰ (zubān-i urdū-yi mu'allā, “language of the royal camp”).

ety: S. पेरव; but cf. H. पेर and P. فريب fareb
def: s.m. & adj. Craftiness, trickery, fraud, deception; — sloping, slanting, crooked, tortuous; crafty, deceitful, fraudulent
s.m. Reproach, reproof.

ety: v.n. fr. اعتراف ěʻtirāf, 'acknowledging,' &c., inf. n. of viii of عرف 'to know,' &c.
def: s.m. Acknowledgment, confession; — goodness; merit; gentleness, lenity; beneficence, favour, kindness, bounty; — (more commonly) the commonly known or received language, common parlance; — adj. Commonly called, commonly known (by the name of, or as), alias; — proper, equitable, just.
root: ا ع ت ر ا ف

def: s.m. Fool, simpleton. (Peculiar to the Dakkhan.)
adj. Well-known, notorious, public.
s.m. pl. The stars; the starry firmament.
s.f. Wailing, pining or crying for
subs: v.n. To cry for an absent person. (Applied to a child only.)

def: s.f. Turning over, upsetting, tilting over
root: ا ڙ ه ک ن ا

def: v.n. To overturn, tilt over, upset.

ety: caus. of اڙهنا uṛhnā, or اوڙهنا oṛhnā
def: v.t. To cover; to put on a covering (as a cloak, shawl, &c.); to clothe, dress; to help to dress.
s.m. = اٿهنگن uṭhangan, and اڙهيکن uṛhīkan, qq.v.
s.m. Covering; clothing ( = oṛhnā).
s.m. Anything placed under a vessel to tilt it up, or to support it; prop; scotch.
s.f. Turning over, upsetting, tilting over
root: ا ڙ ه ک ن ا

def: v.t. = اوڙهنا oṛhnā, q.v.
= A عريان ʻuryān, (v.n. fr. عري 'to be naked,' &c.), Naked, nude, bare, stripped, deprived (of), devoid (of).
adj. Out of debt, free from debt.
adj. Belonging to this side, hithermost.
v.t. To hem; to seam. (See ormā.)

ety: uṛ˚ = Prk. उड्डे(इ) or उड्ड(इ) = S. उड्डीय(ते), rt. उद्+डी
def: v.n. To fly, fly up, soar; to take flight; to move or go rapidly or quickly, to go flying along; to disappear, vanish; to be sprung; to explode (a mine); to spread, become current, be circulated (a report or rumour); to make a show or display, cut a dash; give oneself airs; to be in full bloom, to flourish; to try one's pranks or tricks (with, -se), to practise deceit or fraud (with); to be dexterously or skilfully handled, &c. (an instrument or tune)
subs: v.n. ( = uṛnā, but generally intensive, e.g.), to fly away; to run away, escape; to be wiped out, rubbed out, effaced, obliterated; to be worn out; to be lost; to be exhausted, spent; to disappear, vanish, to be carried off, stolen; to be dissipated; to escape, to evaporate (as scent, &c.); to become faded or dim; to be ravished; lose one's senses; to fly or rush (at, -par)
v.n. To walk with a stately or affected gait, to strut ( = itrānā); to grow vain or conceited (because of, or in, -meṅ)
To be in a state of bloom, to flourish.
